zh.lifeSummary: 當William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness於1410年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安洛斯林時，他的父親Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkney37歲，母親Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkney23歲。他於1432年8月12日與Lady Marjory Sutherland結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1436年登記服兵役。他去世於1482年3月29日在英國蘇格蘭費伏吉卡爾迪享年72歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏吉卡爾迪。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness was born in 1410, in Roslin, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkney, was 37 and his mother, Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkney, was 23. He married Lady Marjory Sutherland on 12 August 1432. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. He registered for military service in 1436. He died on 29 March 1482, in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 72, and was buried in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness est né en 1410, à Roslin, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkney, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkney, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Marjory Sutherland le 12 août 1432. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1436. Il est décédé le 29 mars 1482, à Kirkcaldy, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Kirkcaldy, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat am 12. August 1432 Lady Marjory Sutherland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er hat sich 1436 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 29. März 1482 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithnessが1410年にスコットランドミドロスアンロスリンで生まれた時，父Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkneyは37歳，母Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkneyは23歳であった。1432年8月12日にLady Marjory Sutherlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1436年に兵役登録をした。彼は1482年3月29日にイギリススコットランドフィフキルクカルディで72歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフキルクカルディにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness(이)가 1410년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 로슬린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkney(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkney(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1432년 8월 12일 Lady Marjory Sutherland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1436년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1482년 3월 29일 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 커크캘디에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 커크캘디에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness родился в 1410 г. в местности рослинь, мидлотян, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Henry Sinclair 2nd Earl of Orkney было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Egidia Jill Douglas Countess of Orkney, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Marjory Sutherland 12 августа 1432 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. В 1436 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 29 марта 1482 г. в местности кирккалди, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности кирккалди, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Lady Marjory Sutherland el 12 de agosto de 1432. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1436.
